original_hypothesis.title: Unbounded external hook execution can be used to systematically DoS claim automation by forcing per-winner claim attempts to revert
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: VaultHooks allow winners to register arbitrary contracts that execute before/after prize claims. A malicious hook can revert or consume excessive gas, causing Vault._claimPrize to revert and therefore revert the entire batch in Vault.claimPrizes (and Claimer.claimPrizes). This enables a small set of malicious winners to create persistent operational failures for third-party claimers who batch claims.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Although this is partially a known risk, it warrants operational validation: claimers in production will likely batch claims for cost efficiency. If reverting hooks become common, claim automation can be degraded, skewing claimCount/canaryClaimCount (inputs to tier sizing), delaying prize distribution and changing incentives.

proof_of_signal.pattern: hooks.implementation.beforeClaimPrize(...)/afterClaimPrize(...) external calls without try/catch; nested loops in claimPrizes

preconditions: Winner has set hooks with useBeforeClaimPrize and/or useAfterClaimPrize
Claimer/claimer-bot batches claims including that winner
Hook implementation can revert or be gas-heavy

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Untrusted external calls inside batch processing without isolation.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any draw where malicious-hook winner has prizes to claim.

operational_questions: Do production claimers batch claims across many winners by default?
Is there a fallback strategy to isolate and skip reverting-hook winners automatically?
Do tier expansion metrics degrade if claims are delayed due to hook griefing?
validation_requirements: Measure failure rates and gas consumption for batches including adversarial hooks
Implement and test per-winner isolation strategy in claimer bots
Quantify effect on claimCount/canaryClaimCount and nextNumberOfTiers outcomes

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Configure hooks to always revert in before/after claim
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Wait to win (or observe natural wins)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Cause batch claim txs to revert repeatedly until claimers blacklist the address
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Exploit delayed claiming to skew tier sizing dynamics (secondary effect)

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Automated claiming degraded; increased operational costs; delayed prize payouts

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Sustained across draws if address keeps winning

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Repeated reverts in claim transactions targeting same winner(s)
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Claimers excluding certain winners; increased average claim latency
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Hook contracts with high revert rate

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
